description: Adjudication of a stand-alone 'Open items for adjudication -- Month work-overview & architecture session' docket (D1-D5, drawn from the 2026-06-30 ecosystem review's needs_jordan subset; the docket file itself was never committed to the repo -- pasted verbatim by Jordan and adjudicated against the working tree rather than edited in-place). Verdicts, each independently verified against the actual tree, not just the ledger's self-reported status: D1 (ED-1050, ADEF_THRESHOLD monotonicity) -- RESOLVED, struck; config.py and combat_config.gd confirmed byte-identical monotonic. D2 (ED-1051, module-contract gaps) -- STILL OPEN, context changed: doc:null/[ASSUMPTION] counts grew (11/13, not 10/11), but engine_clock now has a candidate home doc (propagation_spec_v1.md, ED-1093). D3 (ED-1052, typed engine-params direction) -- STILL OPEN, a narrower path found: the export_engine_params.py oracle-export precedent sidesteps the original settled-vs-in-flux dilemma without deciding the broader prose-parsing question. D4 (ED-1054, navigation residual) -- PARTIALLY DONE, narrowed: retired-session-file relocation confirmed complete (ED-1084); narrative-md relocation and stale README regeneration remain open. D5 (freshness pin refresh + blocking flip) -- RESOLVED, struck; valoria-ci.yml confirmed no continue-on-error on the freshness step since the 2026-07-01 LB-23 closure. ACTIONS: cleared ED-1050's stale needs_jordan flag (Jordan already ruled 2026-06-30, flag never cleared alongside the status flip); appended dated addenda to ED-1051/1052/1054 with corrected counts and new context rather than silently re-presenting stale text; decision_queue.md items 5 and 12 annotated, items 24 (D3's broader typed-params-layer question, distinct from item 17's values_master-successor question) and 25 (D4's narrowed navigation residual) added; HANDOFF.md Decisions log entry added. No canon/design content changed -- this is bookkeeping reconciliation of the tracking apparatus itself, allocated its own ED per the ED-1082 precedent (stale-currency-surface reconciliation is its own citable action, not folded silently into whatever ED happens to be open).

Steering-surface reconciliation: workplan v5 §3 says J-38 open/PROPOSED while CURRENT.md L32 + decision_queue item 18 say CANONICAL/RATIFIED (direct contradiction); roadmap_state.yaml (updated 2026-06-28, phase 2, items_done 0) describes a May track divorced from live work; decision-queue items 1-3 reflect pre-R2 combat physics. ACTION: fix J-38 line; regenerate-or-retire roadmap_state; refresh queue items 1-3; adopt the hierarchy CURRENT.md -> lane handoffs -> decision queue authoritative, workplan v5 derived (points at handoffs); consider the monthly reconcile session type (strategic_judgments J-14). RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction on PR #77; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/ed_options.md E-6 (audit S-2)).
resolution_note: EXECUTED with master workplan v6 (ED-IN-0009, PR #78): roadmap_state.yaml retired to deprecated/references/ with banner (regenerate-or-retire -> retire); decision-queue items 1-3 refreshed in place + the queue demoted to a dated snapshot with workplan v6 SS5 as the live decision surface; the v5 J-38 contradiction corrected via v5's supersession banner (J-38 RATIFIED 2026-07-02, ED-1093/ED-1083/ED-1094) and v5 moved to archives/workplans/; hierarchy adopted in v6 SS0: CURRENT.md -> lane handoffs -> workplan (derived) -> dated audits; monthly reconcile institutionalized as v6 SS6 checklist (J-14).

description: Master workplan v6 authored (designs/workplans/valoria_master_workplan_v6.md, CANON-PROPOSED) and the ED-IN-0006 steering reconciliation executed in the same PR (#78). v6: North-Star framing; milestones M1 one-playable-season (7 junctures mapped to delivering lanes) / M2 any-seed story bar / M3 Godot vertical slice; IN spine; narrative-engine workstream per the v2 Churn Engine staging (Stage 2.5 = Layer A alone for M1, hard gate); per-lane workstreams as handoff pointers + next increments (PC R3 U0-U9/T-P2/T5, MB DG-1/DG-2/RC-5 rulings-first, SC stage-ambiguity-first + four games + claim-grammar requirements input, SE/WR/FI/FA/GO); tiered T0/T1/T2 Jordan-decision register with NO status fields (fork/default/blocks/home only); governance incl. the ED-PC plan-text-label collision rule (R3 increments allocate fresh IDs at filing, never trust plan-printed IDs); Appendix A v5 supersession table (every open v5 item accounted; J-37 superseded by the narrative engine; LB-21 moot post-cutover); Appendix B session-inventory snapshot (orphan 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md, 449 findings, zero inbound refs -> one triage ED default). F-F/fork-8 stays HELD BACK from merge-ratification (v2 SS4/SS10). Steering actions: see ED-IN-0006 resolution_note.

Workplan navigator surface built (PR #78): (1) designs/workplans/workplan_v6_progress.yaml — the ONE designated home for milestone/juncture-level rollup status (M1 seven junctures, M2 narrative stages S0-S5, M3 Godot stages), rows = state/next/blocked_on/evidence pointers only, as_of SHA+date; (2) tools/workplan_status.py — sole renderer (one-line summary, --full ASCII progress diagram with you-are-here marker + gates inline, --check staleness vs HEAD; never exits non-zero); (3) skills/valoria-workplan-navigator/SKILL.md — answers 'where are we in the workplan' / 'resume from the workplan': mandatory freshness gate (verify as_of vs HEAD, refresh rows from handoffs/ledger BEFORE answering), position report led by the ASCII diagram + progress-since-last-check delta, ALWAYS ends with 2-4 concrete options (ready-now increment / in-progress thread / Jordan decision sitting / monthly reconcile); (4) SessionStart banner gains a one-line position + staleness flag (tools/session_status.py); Stop hook nudges a board refresh when stale (tools/session_handoff_reminder.py). Anti-rot answer to the retired roadmap_state.yaml (ED-IN-0006): staleness is machine-checked and surfaced, never silently trusted; workplan v6 SS6 names the board + discipline; CLAUDE.md SS9 routing row added.

FULL RATIFICATION of PR #78 per Jordan's explicit instruction 2026-07-05: 'Ratify commit merge all.' Scope: narrative engine v2 (the Churn Engine) RATIFIED incl. the previously HELD-BACK F-F/fork-8 Light-Function decision at its stated default (subtract-only discipline + the enumerated weight set as exposed, versioned authorial parameters — weight VALUES remain tunable data, revisable without re-ratification); design v1 + spec chapters s1-s5 RATIFIED as amended by spec/churn_amendments.md; integration crosswalk RATIFIED; master workplan v6 CANON-RATIFIED (ED-IN-0009). All register forks carrying a stated default adopted at that default: fork 1 (Coup-Counter 1:1 remap onto Löwenritter Autonomy 4-stage — execution remains Stage-1 work), fork 2 (ARC-T04 strike; COLLISION-C -> 7-of-8 — execution remains), fork 3 (window: same-Accounting priors / 4-season cosine discovery), fork 6 (Certainty-in-bake INCLUDE; bake headline ~1,200-2,700), fork 7 (GM-residue + bespoke-EFFECT NPC-ARCs declared non-firing default), fork 9 (reconcile Torben range at Stage-1 compile), fork 11 (scenario_authoring compile = authoring-time), v1 forks 4-5, F-A..F-G (F-G form canonical, numbers still Jordan's later). NOT resolved (no default existed; stay open): fork 10 faction COUNT (spun out as ED-FA-0001), JD-1..8 (PC), DG-1/DG-2/RC-5/ED-1090 (MB), ED-1042, ED-1051, ED-1052, ED-911/WS-7, attribute roster, the Godot strategy register. Status lines flipped in the same merge per ED-1094: v2/v1/amendments/crosswalk, workplan v6 header + §5 ratification update, CURRENT.md rows, HANDOFF surfaces, progress board.

OPT-3, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). F7 smoke oracle: second pinned golden (base_seed=42), named zero-assertions (scenes_resolved/insurgencies_formed/npcs_generated), Hafenmark-lockout assertion, dead-param regression, wall-time ceiling. Lands WITH PR-2's echo wiring so the wiring is born guarded (armature §6.2). RESOLVED 2026-07-08 (F7 oracle PR): F7 smoke oracle landed at sim/tests/test_f7_smoke_oracle.py — pins the n=8/base_seed=42 golden (Varfell 87.5%, the historical small-n artifact, explicitly labelled NOT balance signal), named zero-assertions (scenes_resolved/insurgencies_formed/npcs_generated all 0 across the batch), the Hafenmark elimination-lockout assertion (ED-FA-0005), the VICTORY_THRESHOLD dead-param regression (C-EMERGE-8), and a wall-time ceiling. Minimal additive telemetry added to game_state.World.scenes_resolved + CampaignResult (no behaviour change; the seed-0 golden is unmoved). Landed as a PRE-WIRING BASELINE ahead of the armature echo wiring — the named zero-assertions are designed to TRIP when the transport/keying waves reach these islands, which is the success signal, not a regression. Runs in CI via the existing 'Sim Reference Regression' job (pytest sim/tests).

OPT-7, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Registry hygiene batch: register meta.cascade_cluster_event (C-KEY-8 -- a CANONICAL trigger already cites it); fix scene.gossip's registry entry (the single strict-YAML-parse failure of 48, gap-closure G4 census); unify provenance naming on causes[] (C-INJ-12); reconcile the 44-declared/48-physical type count. The CANONICAL/PROVISIONAL header split on key_type_registry_v30.md was already corrected in this same ruling pass (see the file's header comment, 2026-07-07) -- drop that sub-item from this ED's remaining scope. RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): registered meta.cascade_cluster_event (§8); quoted the scene.gossip scalar (strict-YAML fix); unified provenance naming to causes[]; reconciled the type count 44→48. The A15 rendering-disposition row is deferred to the rendering wave (references/rendering_dispositions.yaml not yet built).

OPT-8, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Consumer-closure batch: add the six ED-935-orphaned npc_behavior consumes edges + refresh its stale gap_note (C-KEY-6); refresh fork-11's scenario_authoring entry (C-INJ-4) and miraculous_event's self-contradictory note (C-INJ-2); correct ED-IN-0014's 'registry §10' citation to module_contracts gap_notes (gap-closure G4). RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): added the six ED-935 npc_behavior consumer edges + refreshed its gap_note; refreshed the fork-11 scenario_authoring gap_note and miraculous_event's self-contradictory note (+ struck the stale PROVISIONAL boilerplate in miraculous_event_v30.md); corrected ED-IN-0014's 'registry §10' citation.

OPT-14, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Intent-hygiene corrections: annotate ED-PC-0001's circular intent sourcing (C-NERSPESS-5) and correct #77 §7's R-2 intent to its own refutation record's UNDETERMINED (C-NERSPESS-6). RULED: approved as ADDENDUM-ONLY corrections -- append a clarifying note to each ratified record, never rewrite or delete the original text (matches the ED-306/append-only discipline for touching ratified surfaces). RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): appended append-only addenda to ED-PC-0001 (C-NERSPESS-5 circular-sourcing) and ners_qualitative_audit_v1.md §7 (C-NERSPESS-6 R-2 intent → UNDETERMINED). Original ratified text left unedited.

OPT-17, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). C-VERIFY carry-forward batch: register back-propagation instances (C-VERIFY-1..4, 22), armor pure-upside (C-VERIFY-5), Domain-Echo season cap (C-VERIFY-12), attr-roster/glossary authority (C-VERIFY-19/21), resonance_style false deprecation (C-VERIFY-20). Partition co-victory un-struck §6.3 (C-VERIFY-23, GD-1-adjacent) sub-item RULED: NO RULE CHANGE -- register as intentionally-unstruck documentation only; a substantive GD-1 propagation question needs its own dedicated design session, not a bundled pick inside this batch. RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): filed doc-notes for C-VERIFY-1/3/4 (mechanics_index staleness), C-VERIFY-2 (three mass-battle supersession_register rows), C-VERIFY-22 (ED-912 path fix), C-VERIFY-12 (Domain-Echo season-cap gap), C-VERIFY-19/21 (glossary IN-FLUX), C-VERIFY-20 (resonance_style correction), C-VERIFY-23 (Partition NO-RULE-CHANGE status note). C-VERIFY-5 (armor cost-free) stays a documented open design question under this ED (no sim change).

Key & Echo Armature PR-2a (executes the IN-lane core of key_echo_armature_v1.md §6.2 'echo transport wiring, flag-gated'; the armature program is ED-IN-0018). Built the DETERMINISTIC echo-transport plumbing that un-orphans domain_echo.py (previously ZERO callers repo-wide, a C-REACH island) and produces the campaign's first canonical substrate KeyLog. New sim/cross_scale/echo_transport.py: make_scheduler() + emit_scene_echo() route a resolved personal/scene outcome -> domain_echo.compute_domain_echo (degree-keyed +-2/+-1/0/-1, Sufficient-Scope gated) -> ONE scene.*_resolved Key emitted through the substrate TickScheduler with an OF-7 DEFERRED faction stat_delta apply that lands at the ACTION->ACCOUNTING boundary. Wired into scene_dispatch._resolve_slot (replaces the zoom_out({}) drop) and mc_v18 (world-scoped KeyLog+scheduler; accounting_boundary()+next_tick() per season; CampaignResult.key_log_hash/keys_emitted telemetry). Gated by an ECHO_TRANSPORT flag (default OFF = byte-exact, MB FIELD_MOVEMENT/ED-1089 precedent); the scheduler's PRESENCE on the world is the flag the scene phase reads. VERIFIED: flag-OFF and flag-ON win-share both byte-identical to the F7 seed-42 golden (Crown 12.5 / Varfell 87.5); OF-7 deferral, degree->delta, and byte-identical replay proven directly in sim/tests/test_echo_transport.py (9 cases). SCOPE DISCIPLINE / DEFERRED (nothing silently dropped): (a) the CONTEXT-DERIVATION bridge (deriving scene actors + which faction/stat is at stake from aggregate world-state) stays SC-lane, ED-SC-0006/0007 — so in the LIVE loop every scene still DEFERS and the plumbing is INERT (KeyLog born empty-but-deterministic, sha256 of ''); it is exercised only by the unit test until that bridge lands. (b) parliamentary_vote-into-the-loop (the Hafenmark comeback) is a BALANCE change owned by FA-lane ED-FA-0005 — NOT wired. (c) §5.5 RNG-model fork NOT engaged (domain_echo takes no rng draw). (d) OF-CAP scheduler caps are caller-supplied per the §5.7 ruling (ED-IN-0026) — no fabricated canonical constant. F7's named-zero-assertions (scenes_resolved/insurgencies/npcs) intentionally stay 0 and flip to feature-assertions when the SC bridge lands. Merge ratifies (ED-1094) — the plumbing is complete; nothing held back.

Built a mobile-friendly, GitHub Pages-hosted status dashboard answering "what has been happening": workplan/roadmap progress (reusing tools/workplan_status.py), recent commit activity (git log grouped by [scope]), a "needs your decision" inbox (workplan T0 items UNIONed with a heuristic scan for JORDAN RULING NEEDED / needs_jordan markers across HANDOFF.md + handoffs/HANDOFF_<LANE>.md), and — the actual gap this closes — a structured registry of audit/simulation-run verdicts (references/audit_registry.jsonl, tools/audit_registry.py) across the 7 audit_type categories (canon_guard, mechanic_audit, resolution_diagnostic, module_adjudicator, vector_audit, editorial_register, simulation_balance), backfilled from designs/audit/ history (tools/build_audit_registry_backfill.py, deliberately conservative: 5 high-confidence entries written, ~148 ambiguous entries skipped rather than guessed) and kept current going forward by retrofitting all 8 audit/simulation skills SKILL.md files with an identical append-on-completion instruction block. tools/ci_audit_registry_check.py is a report-only freshness gate (mirrors ci_editorial_checker.py) warning when a new designs/audit/ folder lacks a registry entry. tools/dashboard_data.py assembles docs/dashboard/data.json; .github/workflows/dashboard.yml builds and deploys docs/dashboard/ to GitHub Pages on push/cron/dispatch. One manual step remains for Jordan: enable Pages once (Settings -> Pages -> Source: GitHub Actions) since the default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot flip that repo setting itself.

IN-lane batch: audit-ecosystem defect fixes (mechanic-audit params path, quantity_registry/descriptor_registry dedup, npc_audit_report_gen path portability, canon_coverage_check GitHub-API->working-tree port, ci_checks_registry known_issues closure). Phase 1 of an audit-ecosystem consolidation plan (session HEAD a95f71d, 2026-07-10). Six fixes, each verified: (1) skills/valoria-mechanic-audit/SKILL.md:21 references/params_<system>.md -> params/<system>.md (the real location; nothing under references/ matches the old pattern), plus rewrote the Output Rules section from imaginary standalone-file outputs to the archives/audit/<date>-<topic>/ dated-folder pattern with an ED-disposition table, matching the convention used throughout archives/audit/. (2) tools/quantity_registry.py's private _load_yaml()/DESCRIPTOR_PATH re-parse of descriptor_registry.yaml replaced with a call into tools/descriptor_registry.py's load() (the file's one parser); normalization/merge/alias logic (resolve(), names_index.yaml merge) unchanged. Verified behavior-preserving: python3 tools/ci_quantity_vocabulary_check.py reports 36 UNRESOLVED both before and after; pytest tests/valoria/test_quantity_vocabulary_check.py tests/valoria/test_names.py passes 18/18 both before and after. (3) tools/observability/npc_audit_report_gen.py's hardcoded Windows C:/Users/Jordan/... and C:/Github/ttrpg/... default paths removed: SRC is now a required CLI arg (no portable default exists -- it named the ephemeral output path of one specific past workflow run), OUT defaults to a repo-relative path derived from __file__ via a three-level _REPO_ROOT (one level deeper than tools/quantity_registry.py's own two-level idiom, since this file lives under tools/observability/); --help added. (4) tools/canon_coverage_check.py ported off the GitHub code-search/contents API (a direct working-tree-is-truth doctrine violation) to a pure os.walk under designs/ matching the literal 'Status: CANONICAL' marker, plus a local read of references/canonical_sources.yaml -- no network calls remain. --strict/--json CLI contract and the designs/audit/ exclusion preserved. Verified: python3 tools/canon_coverage_check.py --strict --json produces valid JSON, exit 1 (drift present), declared_canonical=84 / registered_no_header=28 / unregistered_with_header=1 -- same ballpark as the historical 2026-05-10 41/32 baseline, with corpus drift since expected and fine. CORRECTION (caught by an Opus adversarial review of this same batch, applied before push): the first pass of the os.walk port restricted the file walk to *.md, but registered_paths (line 83) accepts .md/.yaml/.svg -- two real canonical sources, designs/territory/valoria_geography_v30.yaml and valoria_map_v30.svg, carry the Status: CANONICAL marker and were silently dropped from the declared set, producing false-positive registered_no_header drift and contradicting this entry's own "matched set is unchanged" claim. Fixed by matching the walk's extension filter to registered_paths' (.md/.yaml/.svg); counts above are post-fix. (5) references/ci_checks_registry.yaml's three known_issues entries (ci-import-skeleton_gen, freshness_gate-get_live_sha-missing, compliance-doc_index_gen-missing) marked resolved: true with resolved_date + a one-line evidence citation each (freshness_gate.py:80's get_live_sha=get_blob_sha public alias; valoria-ci.yml's syntax-check job already compiling tools/doc_index_gen.py, not skeleton_gen.py; compliance_check.py's _lazy_import() required=[...] list at line 41 resolving doc_index_gen.py correctly before import) -- all three were already fixed in the working tree; only the registry was stale. (6) this entry itself, filed via the documented lane_ids.lanes.IN.next_free protocol in references/id_reservations.yaml (originally allocated as ED-IN-0031 reading next_free=31; RENUMBERED to ED-IN-0032 during a rebase onto main after PR #121 concurrently allocated a different ED-IN-0031 for the same-day dashboard batch -- same failure class documented elsewhere in this ledger for the flat ED-NNNN sequence, resolved the same way: renumber the later-merging side, never overwrite/collide -- co-committed in the same rebase-reconciliation commit).
source: Session-direct defect-fix batch (Phase 1 of an audit-ecosystem consolidation plan); no designs/audit/ artifact produced for this batch -- each fix cites its own file:line evidence in the description above, per CLAUDE.md's anti-fabrication discipline.

description: IN-lane batch: Phase 2 of the audit-ecosystem consolidation plan (session HEAD 05141e6, ED-IN-0032/Phase 1 (post-renumber)). Six items, each verified: (7) tools/mechanics_index_gen.py --strict wired into a new report-only CI job (mechanics-index-check), modeled on the contract-conformance step-level continue-on-error precedent; not listed in ci-summary's needs. Local run against the current tree: 88 mechanics registered, 3 schema errors (mechanics.combat invalid test_status, mechanics.scenario_authoring invalid scale + non-string sim_module), 1 warning -- real pre-existing drift, correctly surfaced report-only rather than blocking on landing. (8) tools/ci_generation_consistency.py wired into a new report-only CI job (generation-consistency-check), closing the local/CI parity gap (it already ran on every local commit via tools/valoria_local.py --staged, one of its 13 checks, but never ran in CI). Confirmed warn-only by design (always returns 0 per its own docstring/return 0 at end of main()); continue-on-error added anyway for consistency with sibling report-only jobs. Local run: 93 canonical docs checked, 34 lacking a Status line, 1 recorded as superseded (designs/scene/combat_v30.md, already known/documented drift). (9) tools/canon_coverage_check.py --strict --json wired into a new report-only CI job (canon-coverage-check), per Jordan's ruling, immediately following its Phase-1 (ED-IN-0031) doctrine-violation fix. Confirmed locally: exits 1 (real drift: 1 unregistered_with_header, 28 registered_no_header), which is expected/fine for a report-only wire. (10) tools/observability/social_contest_audit.workflow.js retired to deprecated/tools/observability/ via git mv, per Jordan's ruling, with a header comment recording why: never executed (no designs/audit/ artifact anywhere cites the workflow by name, confirmed by grep); its CORPUS constant cited sim/personal/contest.py, which no longer exists (the 2026-07-02 Stage 3/Gate C rebuild, PR #63, replaced it with the sim/personal/contest/ package); its CANON_NOTE source-hierarchy predates the ED-SC-0002..0015 ruling sequence; the SC lane already has a ratified, executed Fable-5 subsystem audit covering this ground (designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/, PR #80). (11) references/ci_checks_registry.yaml regenerated: ci_checks entries 8 -> 22, covering every tools/*.py invoked directly in .github/workflows/valoria-ci.yml (verified by grep: 22 distinct `python3 tools/X.py` invocations, all now present), each with level/role/paired_hook/ci_job/notes in the file's existing terse style; canon_coverage_check.py's ci_job flipped from empty to canon-coverage-check to reflect item 9. Added a new mechanical verifier, tools/broken_dependency_checker.py::check_ci_registry_coverage() (stdlib-only, no new PyYAML dependency in the integrity job), wired into the existing 'Broken dependency check' step of the repository-integrity job (BLOCKING, matching its neighbors): (a) every registry path: exists on disk, (b) every non-empty ci_job: names a real job id in valoria-ci.yml, (c) every python3 tools/X.py invocation in valoria-ci.yml has a matching registry path: entry, (d) every non-empty paired_hook: exists as a def in valoria_hooks.py -- found at deprecated/skills/valoria-orchestrator/scripts/valoria_hooks.py (retired but still literally present, so the check runs rather than being skipped; all existing paired_hook values resolve cleanly against it). Verified: 0 violations against the regenerated registry; deliberately broke one path/ci_job/paired_hook value at a time and confirmed the verifier catches each, then confirmed clean again after reverting. (12) tools/README.md regenerated: CI Tools and Integrity Tools tables now sourced from the regenerated ci_checks_registry.yaml (documented as downstream of it, not independently maintained); a hand-written Utility Tools section lists the ~18 non-checker scripts the registry doesn't cover by design (generators, one-off executors, shared libraries, and the three Claude Code-native local hooks wired via .claude/settings.json). This entry itself filed via the documented lane_ids.lanes.IN.next_free protocol in references/id_reservations.yaml (originally allocated as ED-IN-0032 reading next_free=32; RENUMBERED to ED-IN-0033 during the same rebase-onto-main reconciliation that renumbered Phase 1's ED-IN-0031 -> ED-IN-0032, after PR #121 concurrently claimed ED-IN-0031 -- see that entry and the id_reservations.yaml IN-lane comment for the full incident).
source: Session-direct wire/retire/regenerate batch (Phase 2 of an audit-ecosystem consolidation plan, following ED-IN-0031's Phase 1); no designs/audit/ artifact produced for this batch -- each item cites its own file:line/job-id evidence in the description above, per CLAUDE.md's anti-fabrication discipline.

description: IN-lane batch: Phase 3 of the audit-ecosystem consolidation plan (session HEAD 806aa63, ED-IN-0032/Phase 2). Full rewrite of skills/valoria-editorial-register/SKILL.md, which still documented a single canon/editorial_ledger.yaml with a # next_id: header and an invented 17-field schema -- a structure that stopped existing at the 2026-05-28 YAML->JSONL migration and the 2026-07-02 ED-<LANE>-NNNN lane cutover (ED-IN-0001), three months and one full restructure before this fix. Rewritten against ground truth read directly from the working tree: the real store (canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl frozen-flat + eight/nine canon/editorial_ledger_<lane>.jsonl lane files + _archive.jsonl, all JSONL); the real ID law (ED-<LANE>-NNNN via references/id_reservations.yaml's lane_ids.lanes.<LANE>.next_free protocol, worked examples cited from this session's own ED-IN-0031/0032 commits 05141e6/806aa63); the real field vocabulary (observed-descriptive, not enforced -- explicitly documents that e.g. every ED-MB-* entry adds resolution/date_resolved but ED-FI-0003/0004 resolve with neither field, folding the date into description prose instead); and a new 'Enforcement reality' section distinguishing the two unrelated CI mechanisms that actually touch this system -- tools/ci_editorial_checker.py (checks for [EDITORIAL:]/[PROVISIONAL:] markers in designs/npcs//world//arcs/gm_ref/ + canon/03_canonical_timeline.md commits; does NOT parse the ledger at all) versus tools/broken_dependency_checker.py::check_editorial_ledger (the BLOCKING repository-integrity check that actually reads every lane file and validates live-status entries' file-path references against the working tree). Doc-only -- no .jsonl file or code touched by the rewrite itself; this entry is the one exception, filed separately after the doc commit to keep the audit trail consistent with Phase 1/2's pattern, originally via the documented lane_ids.lanes.IN.next_free protocol reading next_free=33 (id ED-IN-0033 at filing time); RENUMBERED to ED-IN-0034 during the same rebase-onto-main reconciliation that renumbered Phase 1 (ED-IN-0031->0032) and Phase 2 (ED-IN-0032->0033), after PR #121 concurrently claimed ED-IN-0031 -- see that entry and the id_reservations.yaml IN-lane comment for the full incident (next_free 34 -> 35).
source: skills/valoria-editorial-register/SKILL.md (rewritten, commit 4ddb655); canon/editorial_ledger_fi.jsonl / _mb.jsonl / _wr.jsonl (read as ground truth for the field-vocabulary table); tools/ci_editorial_checker.py, tools/broken_dependency_checker.py (read for the Enforcement reality section)

description: IN-lane batch: Phase 5b of the audit-ecosystem consolidation plan (following ED-IN-0032/0033/0034, Phases 1-3). New .github/workflows/audit-refresh.yml -- the repo's first maintenance use of schedule: (monthly cron) + workflow_dispatch: (on-demand) + $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, mirroring the pattern PR #121's dashboard.yml introduced the same week. One job: decisions-digest-refresh regenerates tools/observability/decisions.json/DECISIONS.md/decisions_data.js via tools/observability/build_decisions.py (fully deterministic, no LLM) and opens a PR if the corpus scan changed anything -- never pushes directly to main. Not added to valoria-ci.yml's ci-summary needs: it is maintenance, not a merge gate. SCOPE CORRECTION from the original plan: a second job was designed to re-run skills/valoria-vector-audit/scripts/vector_audit.py's deterministic corpus-extraction/diagnostic stages on the same schedule. Investigating that script before building the job found it is a STUB -- main() has no stage dispatcher at all ('Stage execution dispatcher would go here'); the actual implementation that produced archives/audit/2026-04-29-topographic-analysis/'s data/*.json files was never ported into it and exists nowhere else in the repo. Building a CI job to 'run' a pipeline that doesn't exist would silently commit near-empty output while looking like real automation, undermining tools/audit_staleness.py's own honest 'vector-audit: 412 files changed since last refresh' signal (Phase 5a, same plan). Deferred, not built: vector_audit.py needs its stage dispatcher actually implemented -- a real engineering task -- before any automated refresh of it is possible. This also means Phase 6 item 13 (a vector-audit v4 rerun) is more blocked than the plan assumed: it needs the pipeline implemented first, not just a methodology-delta note.
source: .github/workflows/audit-refresh.yml (new); skills/valoria-vector-audit/scripts/vector_audit.py (read in full -- confirmed stub, no stage dispatcher); archives/audit/2026-04-29-topographic-analysis/ (confirmed no re-runnable script produced this data)

description: IN-lane batch: Phase 6 (partial) of the audit-ecosystem consolidation plan (following ED-IN-0032/0033/0034/0035, Phases 1/2/3/5b). Two items: (14) forward-only findings-disposition discipline added to two more audit skills' output contracts -- skills/valoria-vector-audit/SKILL.md (02_weakness_register.md findings must resolve to a filed ED-<LANE>-NNNN id or an explicit no-action line, applies going forward only) and skills/valoria-simulator/SKILL.md (same discipline added to Mode D Edge Case Discovery's output, right after its sim_edge_cases_[domain].md output line) -- matching the pattern already added to valoria-mechanic-audit (ED-IN-0032, Phase 1). While editing vector-audit's SKILL.md, corrected two more stale references discovered in passing: its Reference Files section claimed scripts/vector_audit.py is 'full pipeline, runs all stages' -- false, per ED-IN-0035's stub finding -- now documents the stub accurately with a pointer to that finding; and its Cross-references section cited designs/audit/2026-04-29-topographic-analysis/ three times, which does not exist -- the real location is archives/audit/2026-04-29-topographic-analysis/ (confirmed by directory listing), all three corrected. Item 13 (an actual vector-audit v4 rerun) remains explicitly deferred -- blocked on vector_audit.py's stage dispatcher actually being implemented (ED-IN-0035), a real engineering task out of scope for this plan. Item 15 (a full-corpus canon-guard sweep) was skipped per Jordan's ruling earlier this session (per-mechanic gating suffices; a sweep would dump more findings into an already-large open-decision backlog).
source: skills/valoria-vector-audit/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-simulator/SKILL.md (both edited); archives/audit/2026-04-29-topographic-analysis/ (directory listing, confirms the correct path); ED-IN-0035 (the stub finding this batch cites and documents in-skill)

description: RENUMBERED from its original ED-IN-0032 (read at next_free=32) during a rebase reconciling onto main after PR #122's audit-ecosystem consolidation batch concurrently claimed ED-IN-0032 through ED-IN-0036 for its own six phases -- same failure class documented elsewhere in this ledger, resolved the same way: the later-merging side renumbers (this PR was still open when PR #122 merged first). Extended the ED-IN-0031 status dashboard per Jordan feedback ("dashboard is supposed to show me most recent game victory balances, most recent balance matrices for subsystems" + "show the audit results and all the other items that keep tracking work like registers and stuff... recording stuff from simulations and sims and tests"). Researched what structured (non-prose) balance/victory data actually exists on disk before building anything, per the anti-fabrication discipline (CLAUDE.md §7) -- found no consistent convention and most subsystems have none at all. Added: (1) a Balance & victory data card -- personal_combat weapon x armour win-rate matrix (designs/audit/2026-05-29-combat-armature/weapon_rebalance_data.json, flagged STALE: predates the 2026-07-02 40-weapon morphology expansion, no regenerated matrix exists since), faction_political win-share (AST-parsed GOLDEN_WIN_SHARE constants from sim/tests/test_mc_v18_regression.py [seed 0, n=2] and sim/tests/test_f7_smoke_oracle.py [seed 42, n=8], labeled as CI-pinned regression guards, not a persisted full-campaign run), and explicit no-data flags for mass_battle/social_contest/threadwork/settlement_territory (verified no structured file exists for any of them). (2) A Registers card -- editorial ledger open/needs_jordan counts per lane (142 open, 33 needs_jordan across all lanes) and active patch-register counts by status, read directly from canon/editorial_ledger*.jsonl and canon/patch_register_active.yaml. (3) Corrected the dashboard's own simulation_balance callout, which had been repeating the now-debunked "~87% degenerate win-share, nothing flags it" claim (CLAUDE.md §7) verbatim -- sim/tests/test_f7_smoke_oracle.py's own docstring explains this was a small-N artifact of one unguarded run_batch(8, seed=42), since replaced by two guarded goldens; the dashboard now states the corrected framing instead of propagating the debunked one.

Skills-ecosystem staleness remediation, Phase 7 (continuing the 2026-07-11 audit-ecosystem batch, ED-IN-0032..0037/Phases 1-6, which had already fully rewritten valoria-editorial-register and partially touched valoria-vector-audit/valoria-simulator). A fresh independent audit (3 parallel research agents covering all 15 live skills, cross-checked against CLAUDE.md's current architecture) found residual staleness the earlier phases did not reach. Fixed: valoria-simulator (Mode G Step 2 checkpoints pointed at monolithic HANDOFF.md; now point at handoffs/HANDOFF_<LANE>.md); valoria-arc-generator (dangling copy-paste 'Exit 0 required on all three' line with no antecedent, removed); valoria-atomizer (dead 'See valoria-orchestrator skill Collision Prevention' citation to a retired skill, inlined instead; Priority Order still targeted designs/scene/combat_v30.md, which CLAUDE.md itself flags PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED -- replaced with a CURRENT.md-resolution instruction and removed combat_v30.md from the list; Pre-Commit Checks was missing tools/ci_co_file_checker.py, the actual CI-enforced index/infill co-file gate); references/design_registry.yaml's combat entry (still pointed canonical_v30/params at the superseded doc and a nonexistent params/combat.md -- added a superseded-notes banner, nulled params). The dominant repeated defect: three skills (valoria-mechanic-audit Mode D, valoria-module-adjudicator Stage 3, valoria-resolution-diagnostic Stage 3 NERS verdict output) each independently instructed findings to append to the FROZEN flat canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl instead of the live lane-split canon/editorial_ledger_<lane>.jsonl files (CLAUDE.md section 3's 2026-07-02 cutover) -- a defect pattern Phase 1's narrower 'mechanic-audit params path fix' (ED-IN-0032) did not catch, since it addressed a different issue. module-adjudicator additionally cited a dead append_to_register function that only ever existed in the retired orchestrator's github_ops.py. Also fixed: mechanic-audit's Output Rules pointed new findings at archives/audit/ (history-only per CLAUDE.md section 1) instead of designs/audit/; workplan-navigator's decisions pointer cited only the flat ledger (missing the lane-split files) and had no awareness of the 2026-07-11 mobile dashboard/'needs your decision' inbox (added both). valoria-compiler received the deepest rework: fixed a nonexistent canon/editorial_ledger.yaml citation, removed a dead compilation_current gate (canonical_sources.yaml carries no such field), retired the phantom compilation/v[N]/... output path (nothing in the live repo reads or writes it) in favor of two real modes -- in-place ratification per the ED-1094 merge-ratifies-by-default convention (flip Status line + ED ledger + CURRENT.md same commit), or an explicit full export to a dated designs/audit/ folder -- and removed the same dangling exit-0 line. Full findings and the skills found already CURRENT (prose-writer, valoria-chunker, valoria-canon-guard, valoria-vector-audit, valoria-editorial-register) are in designs/audit/2026-07-12-skills-ecosystem-audit/.
source: skills/valoria-simulator/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-arc-generator/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-atomizer/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-mechanic-audit/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-module-adjudicator/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-resolution-diagnostic/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-workplan-navigator/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-compiler/SKILL.md, references/design_registry.yaml (all edited); designs/audit/2026-07-12-skills-ecosystem-audit/ (full report)

description: Retired valoria-combat-simulator to deprecated/skills/ (mirroring the valoria-orchestrator precedent). Its bundled scripts/combat_sim.py was a hand-hardcoded 9-weapon model (Short/Long x Light/Heavy Cut/Blunt) frozen 2026-03-31, fully superseded by designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/workbench/balance.py -- an actively-maintained, 51-weapon (40 added in the 2026-07-02 morphology expansion, plus the original 11), Wilson-CI-validated balance harness that reuses the canonical fight() resolver directly (weapon_matchup_table/attribute_parity_table/tradition_field_table/weapon_armour_matrix sweeps) instead of a hand-maintained parallel model. The skill's own Mandatory Input Validation additionally pointed at params/combat.md, deprecated since 2026-06-04 (references/canonical_sources.yaml), and its patch-proposal format used a non-conforming PP-SIM-NNN id that never matched canon/patch_register_active.yaml's plain PP-NNN scheme or id_reservations.yaml's counters. Decision confirmed with Jordan via AskUserQuestion before executing (retire, vs. minimal-patch, vs. rearchitect-to-wrap-balance.py). Updated: deprecated/skills/README.md (new entry), CLAUDE.md section 9 routing table (Combat-balance simulation row now points directly at workbench/balance.py) + a retirement note alongside the existing valoria-orchestrator one, references/effort-guide.md (dropped the combat-simulator row; also dropped the already-stale valoria-orchestrator row found in the same table, incidental cleanup). ADVERSARIAL-PASS ADDENDUM (2026-07-12, same PR): an independent critic found the "40-weapon" figure conflated weapons-added with weapons-total (corrected above to 51 total) and that workbench/balance.py's CLI has no mode for the retired skill's full pairwise build-vs-build cross-product (the underlying winrate() function supports it; not wired to the CLI) -- noted in deprecated/skills/README.md as a known, minor, non-blocking coverage gap rather than silently claiming full parity.
source: deprecated/skills/valoria-combat-simulator/ (moved), deprecated/skills/README.md, CLAUDE.md, references/effort-guide.md (all edited); designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/workbench/balance.py (verified as the superseding instrument, read in full)

description: valoria-dice-model: added the canonical continuous (Godot-mode) resolver alongside the existing legacy-TTRPG discrete d10 model, per Jordan's confirmation via AskUserQuestion. Prior state: valoria_dice.py implemented only the discrete pool model and the SKILL.md presented it flatly as 'Current standard' with no acknowledgment that params/core.md section Continuous Engine (Decision E, 2026-05-15) designates net ~ Normal(mu*N, sigma*sqrt(N)) as canonical for the videogame/Godot implementation specifically, with the discrete rule demoted to legacy/TTRPG-mode only. Added continuous_outcome_probs(n, tn, ob) and continuous_quick_check(n, tn, ob) to valoria_dice.py -- closed-form via the normal CDF (math.erf, no scipy dependency), continuity-corrected (resolves against x - 0.5 per the ER-2 fix, params/core.md commit a3d3888) so odds track the discrete model at small pools, matching params/core.md's own Degrees of Success thresholds (Overwhelming net >= max(2*Ob,3), Success net >= Ob, Partial 0<net<Ob, Failure net<=0) and supporting fractional Ob (canonical in videogame mode). Validated by direct comparison against the existing discrete Monte Carlo implementation at n=4/8/12, TN7, Ob3: continuous p_full within 0.003-0.008 of discrete across all three pool sizes, consistent with params/core.md's own claimed max-deviation-0.029 equivalence. SKILL.md rewritten with a 'Two Resolver Modes' section framing which mode governs which context (TTRPG-facing vs. Godot-facing questions), a new Task 9 (continuous-mode table), and removal of a dangling params/combat.md row from the Params File Reference table (that file does not exist, deprecated 2026-06-04).
source: skills/valoria-dice-model/valoria_dice.py, skills/valoria-dice-model/SKILL.md (both edited); params/core.md section Continuous Engine (primary source cited); skills/valoria-resolution-diagnostic/SKILL.md Instance A (secondary corroboration of the same formula, already-correct prior art)

description: Closed a cheap ecosystem gap surfaced by the Phase 7 audit (ED-IN-0038): PP-NNN mechanical-patch allocation had no documented protocol anywhere, unlike ED-<LANE>-NNNN's thorough ID Law/Collision Guard section in valoria-editorial-register. references/id_reservations.yaml already reserves PP blocks per round with the identical next_free collision-guard mechanism EDs use (e.g. round D: PP 800-829, next_free 810) -- the gap was documentation, not missing infrastructure. Added a 'PP Number Collision Guard' section to skills/valoria-editorial-register/SKILL.md mirroring the existing ED section's structure: re-read id_reservations.yaml's live PP next_free immediately before assigning, form the entry as plain PP-NNN (no lane tag -- PP predates and was never folded into the ED lane split), append to canon/patch_register_active.yaml, bump and co-commit id_reservations.yaml in the same commit. Also referenced from valoria-compiler's patch-application step. The one live non-conforming format found (valoria-combat-simulator's PP-SIM-NNN) was closed by retiring that skill entirely (ED-IN-0039) rather than reformatting a dead reference.
source: skills/valoria-editorial-register/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-compiler/SKILL.md (both edited); references/id_reservations.yaml (verified PP block structure, read only, no allocation made by this entry itself)

description: Adversarial verification pass on PR #126 (ED-IN-0038..0042), requested by Jordan ('Please perform adversarial pass') and scoped via AskUserQuestion to verifying the PR's own changes rather than a fresh broader audit. Three independent read-only critic agents, each blind to the producer's reasoning, re-derived every claim directly from the working tree rather than trusting the commit summary -- following the repo's own agonist/antagonist relay pattern (CLAUDE.md section 10). Also independently re-verified no concurrent ED-IN allocation collision occurred (fetched origin/main live: still at 847c375, next_free still 43 pre-this-entry, identical to the base this PR read). Findings, all fixed in this same PR before merge: (1) real correctness bug -- continuous_outcome_probs() in valoria_dice.py did not implement params/core.md's documented Ob-20 exception (Overwhelming unavailable; Partial requires net>=10), producing wrong probabilities at realistic pool sizes (e.g. n=100 TN7 Ob20 gave overwhelming=0.525 instead of the documented 0.0) -- implemented the special case and corrected the docstring/SKILL.md overclaim that thresholds matched 'exactly'. (2) A repeated factual inaccuracy -- 'combat_engine_v1 has a 40-weapon roster' -- actually 51 total (40 added in the 2026-07-02 morphology expansion, plus the original 11); the code comment says '40 weapons added', which three docs (CLAUDE.md, deprecated/skills/README.md, this ledger's own ED-IN-0039 entry, and handoffs/HANDOFF_IN.md) misread as 'total'. Corrected in all four. (3) A genuine, if minor, capability-coverage gap in the combat-simulator retirement -- workbench/balance.py's CLI exposes only curated sweep modes, not the retired skill's full pairwise build-vs-build matrix (the underlying winrate() function supports it; not wired to a CLI mode) -- documented honestly in deprecated/skills/README.md rather than silently claiming full parity. (4) Two intra-PR misses where a file this PR itself edited still carried a defect of the exact class the PR was fixing elsewhere: skills/valoria-arc-generator/SKILL.md:26 still cited the dead compilation_current field, and references/design_registry.yaml's next_id_note comment still cited the nonexistent canon/editorial_ledger.yaml -- both fixed. (5) A minor miscounted inline comment (id_reservations.yaml said ED-IN-0038 touched '9 skills', actually 8) -- corrected. Everything else across all three critics' scope (the lane-split ledger fixes in mechanic-audit/module-adjudicator/resolution-diagnostic, the PP-NNN protocol addition, the compiler rework, the atomizer fixes, workplan-navigator's dashboard awareness, the 5 original ED-IN entries' JSON validity and non-collision, the combat-simulator move's completeness) was independently CONFIRMED, not merely re-asserted. Noted but explicitly NOT re-litigated: pre-existing, PR-external references to the nonexistent canon/editorial_ledger.yaml remain scattered across several live (non-archived) canonical docs (references/glossary.md, designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md, designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md, and others) -- real debt, but outside this PR's stated scope (all 15 skills/*/SKILL.md files), not introduced by it, and not silently claimed as fixed; left for separate future cleanup.
source: skills/valoria-dice-model/valoria_dice.py, skills/valoria-dice-model/SKILL.md, skills/valoria-arc-generator/SKILL.md, references/design_registry.yaml, references/id_reservations.yaml, CLAUDE.md, deprecated/skills/README.md, handoffs/HANDOFF_IN.md (all edited this entry); designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/workbench/balance.py, params/core.md (read, cited as ground truth by the critics)

description: Repo-wide sweep of every engine/resolver/simulator/test/Python surface (sim/ 104 files, tests/ 206 files across tests/valoria [real, CI-gated, 141 passed], sim/tests [real ED-1053 regression oracle, 419 passed], tests/contracts [34 passed], tests/sim + tests/sim_framework [both dead/archival despite the name collision with sim/ -- disambiguated per sim/README.md], plus ~2.5MB of non-executable narrative-audit markdown; tools/ 44 files; the 8 audit/simulation skills) to evaluate simulation/testing methodology and propose a harness. Builds directly on PR #122's audit-ecosystem consolidation (ED-IN-0032..0037, merged 2026-07-11), which fixed the audit-TOOLING layer but explicitly left the audit_registry.jsonl live-logging gap and any simulation-execution harness untouched (see ED-IN-0035). Findings: contract_adjudicator.py (the one real A1-A12 contract-closure linter) is unit-tested but never run against the live module_contracts.yaml by CI; combat_sim.py/valoria_dice.py produce real Monte Carlo numbers with no asserted CI thresholds, only human prose judgment; 5 of 8 audit skills have zero executable backing; audit_registry.jsonl has exactly 5 entries, all backfilled, none ever live-appended by an actual skill run despite every skill claiming to; tests/hooks/, tests/index/, tests/registry/, and 2 files under tests/sim/ contain real pytest code no CI job or local hook executes; sim/provincial/massbattle.py (1905 LOC, CANONICAL) has zero dedicated unit test; sim/personal/combat.py is confirmed dead (DEPRECATED banner, 2026-06-23) but remains importable. Proposal filed at designs/audit/2026-07-12-simulation-test-harness-methodology/simulation_test_harness_methodology_v1.md (PROPOSED): a single generic harness core (canon-parameter resolver bound to CURRENT.md, never fabricates) plus one thin per-module Adapter (the modular 'test module' requested) bound to module_contracts.yaml's existing IN->resolver->OUT shape rather than a new interface; a depth-tiered (1 minor/2 medium/3 major) probabilistic branch-exploration policy per resolver-call 'event'; and a mandatory triage-flag taxonomy (CANON_GAP/CONTRACT_VIOLATION/OUT_OF_RANGE/STUB_HIT/DEGENERATE_DISTRIBUTION/FABRICATION_RISK) that can never be silently swallowed into a PASS verdict. A minimal Gate-0 prototype ships alongside the doc at tools/sim_harness/ (harness core + logger + one demo adapter over valoria_dice.py, deliberately the simplest, lowest-game-balance-risk resolver in the repo) -- confirmed runnable, and its own audit_registry.jsonl append in this same commit is the first LIVE (non-backfilled) entry the registry has ever received, concretely demonstrating the fix rather than only proposing it. Rollout order (design doc section 8): dice model -> combat_sim -> social contest kernel -> mass battle -> full mc_v18 campaigns, each wave report-only in CI before promotion to blocking, mirroring PR #122's own ratchet pattern. Four independent quick-win findings (section 9) are cheap and separable from the architecture decision: wire or retire the three orphaned real-pytest directories; guard against sim/personal/combat.py accidental reimport; retire tools/propagator.py/find_references.py/verify_cuts.py (same orphaned-tool profile as the 2026-07-09 batch, missed by that sweep's exact heuristics); wire contract_adjudicator.py into CI report-only independent of the rest of this proposal. NEEDS JORDAN (called out per CLAUDE.md section 2's loud-exception rule, not routine work to ratify on merge): the section-8 rollout order (mass battle has the largest raw coverage gap but is deliberately sequenced fourth, not first); whether wave 1's CI job must go through the full report-only burn-in every other checker used or can block sooner; whether a full-campaign mc_v18 harness run should ever gate a PR given its cost/blast-radius; and whether the four quick wins should land in this same PR or be filed as separate lane work.
source: designs/audit/2026-07-12-simulation-test-harness-methodology/simulation_test_harness_methodology_v1.md; tools/sim_harness/ (new); references/audit_registry.jsonl (live append); CLAUDE.md sections 1/2/6/7/8 (currency, ratification, Godot pipeline, sim, enforcement); references/module_contracts.yaml; sim/README.md; four parallel research-agent inventories of sim/, tests/, tools/, and the 8 audit skills
resolution: RATIFIED 2026-07-12. The 4 open questions (design doc section 11) were put to Jordan directly via AskUserQuestion, not assumed. Answers: (1) rollout order -- Jordan flagged a real gap ("Where is settlement management, faction actions, field investigations, threadwork?"); section 8 extended to add faction_action.py, sim/territory/*, and sim/thread/* as waves 5-7 (mass battle stays wave 4, campaign composition now wave 8); field investigation explicitly excluded, not omitted -- its sim/ implementation is still [PROVISIONAL] stub-only, no real resolver to adapt yet. (2) Wave 1 CI burn-in: "Full report-only burn-in" -- existing ratchet convention applies, no deviation. (3) mc_v18.py as CI gate: "Never gate a PR" -- firm constraint on the eventual campaign adapter. (4) four section-9 quick-win findings: "File separately" -- filed as ED-IN-0045 rather than bundled into this PR. Between initial filing and ratification, the Gate-0 prototype went through six rounds of adversarial review + deliberate stress-testing (not part of the original filing) that found and fixed 34 real bugs -- see tools/sim_harness/README.md for the full account. PR jordanelias/ttrpg#124. COLLISION 2026-07-12, resolved by renumbering the later-merging side (same pattern documented elsewhere in this ledger): this PR (#124, branch claude/repo-audit-testing-methodology-w2f1wo) originally allocated ED-IN-0038/0039 for the simulation-harness methodology + prototype, but a separate PR (#126, "Skills-ecosystem staleness remediation, Phase 7") independently claimed ED-IN-0038 through ED-IN-0043 and merged to main first. Renumbered ED-IN-0038 -> ED-IN-0044 (this entry) and ED-IN-0039 -> ED-IN-0045 (the quick-wins follow-up) during the merge reconciling onto main.

description: Repo-realignment program WS0a: implemented the vector_audit.py stage dispatcher, superseding the ED-IN-0035/0036 STUB status (main() was a no-op printing a pointer; PR #131's vector_audit step came back BLOCKED because of it). Now implements Stages 1-6 per references/methodology.md + diagnostic_modes.md over the WORKING TREE (no GitHub fetch, CLAUDE.md SS2): Stage 1 corpus extraction + banner classifier (reads design_doc/params paths from canonical_sources.yaml + canon/00-03 + throughlines), Stage 2 seed-token curation (reuses the in-file SEED_TOKENS/PILOT_TOKENS/CLASSES/helpers) with SS3.5 disambiguation, Stage 2.5 G_cite (mentions-in-primary-doc >= 2), Stage 4 G_throughline/G_mu (throughlines_meta_infill) + G_pp (patch_register_active affects), Stage 3 G_tfidf (sklearn, SUPPORTING-ONLY and OPTIONAL -- numpy/sklearn absent degrades gracefully so it runs in CI/local with pyyaml only), Stage 5 P1/P2/P3 structural validation (in+out neighbour union), Stage 6 the 8 diagnostic modes (A hubs / B implied-missing / C notional / D cascade-sinks / E sparse / F throughline-orphan / G vocab-debt / H isolates). Writes data/*.json + 02_weakness_register.md + 03_validation_report.md. First working-tree run (2026-07-13): 108 design docs, 74 tokens, 2258 cite-edges (P3 PASS), validation FAILED 1/3 (P2 conviction-symmetry fails -- a real finding per methodology SS3.8, not a bug). The 3 new axis-graphs (G_pointer/G_generation/G_code) + methodology-v4 revalidation are deferred to WS0b (later, alongside their WS1/WS2/WS3 dependencies). SKILL.md stub-warning language updated to match.
source: repo-realignment plan WS0a; run verified 2026-07-13 against the working tree
